summary: End-to-end testing of AE Compatibility Converter (backend API + frontend UI). All 10 backend pytest cases pass; full frontend E2E flow (file set → version select → convert → results → download buttons) passes. No critical or minor issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: converter.py: _scan_counts is case-insensitive for matchnames (re.IGNORECASE) which matches Adobe match-name conventions (always uppercase) but could over-count if user data text accidentally contains a similar substring; current implementation acceptable given Adobe matchname conventions.
server.py _load_job: missing try/except around json.loads — if report.json is corrupted will surface 500. Low severity since files are written atomically by us.

server.py: CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will reject credentialed requests. App doesn't use credentials so functionally fine but worth tightening.
Frontend Converter.jsx: no toast/error UI for invalid target_version since UI only allows 2020-2023, low risk; no loading skeleton for results, but processing button state covers it.
seed_data_creation: Tests fetch /api/sample for sample .aepx and construct an in-memory .aepx in the Playwright run; no DB seed required (no persistence).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_converter_api.py — covers /api/sample, /api/convert happy path (2020), 2020 vs 2023 removal delta, rejections (.txt, empty, invalid target), /api/download content + version metadata + XML validity, /api/download/{id}/zip contents, 404, and .aep binary detection-only. Frontend E2E uses page.set_input_files on data-testid=file-input then clicks version-option-2020 and convert-button to populate results-panel with removed-effect-*, replaced-effect-*, warning-*, download-button, download-zip-button data-testids.
